2007-10-01.title: Toxic Toothpaste
2007-10-01.abstract: How a Panamanian man's discovery triggered a global hunt for tainted toothpaste manufactured in China.

2007-10-01.title: Plan to Disarm North Korea Is Evaluated as Talks Recess
2007-10-01.abstract: A draft agreement on dismantling North Korea’s nuclear program is expected to be made public after a two-day recess in talks.

2007-10-01.title: Many Czechs Love U.S., but Say ‘Hold the Radar’
2007-10-01.abstract: The Czech population is deeply divided over whether its country should host a controversial U.S. missile-defense radar system that is opposed by Russia.

2007-10-01.title: Iraqi Violence Ebbed in September, Reports Say
2007-10-01.abstract: Last month saw declines in the rate of violent civilian deaths in Iraq and in the number of U.S. forces killed.

2007-10-01.title: U.N. Envoy Tries to Ease Tensions in Myanmar
2007-10-01.abstract: A U.N. envoy met Sunday with opposition leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi and with members of Myanmar’s military junta that crushed pro-democracy protests last week.

2007-10-02.title: Brutality in Congo
2007-10-02.abstract: Jeffrey Gettleman reports from Panzi Hospital in Congo, where nearly all of the patients are survivors of brutal rapes.

2007-10-02.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 12 in Kabul
2007-10-02.abstract: A suicide bomber approached a bus carrying police officers and civilian employees of the Interior Ministry and blew himself up, killing at least 12 people.

2007-10-02.title: Momentum Seems to Build for an Independent Kosovo
2007-10-02.abstract: The world powers that will help determine Kosovo’s fate are again pushing to separate it completely from Serbia despite continued resistance from the Serbs and the Russians.

2007-10-02.title: Police Officials Suspended in Pakistan Over Battle With Lawyers
2007-10-02.abstract: On Saturday, dozens of lawyers were beaten as they protested a bid for re-election by the president, Gen. Pervez Musharraf.

2007-10-02.title: Peacekeepers as Targets: Darfur Attack Imperils Talks
2007-10-02.abstract: The deadly attack on a peacekeeping base in Darfur late Saturday threatened to undermine fragile efforts by the U.N. and the African Union to forge a peace agreement.

2007-10-03.title: North Korean Nuclear Talks Close to Consensus
2007-10-03.abstract: Negotiators reached agreement on a draft in Beijing on Sunday after four days of six-nation talks on dismantling North Korea’s nuclear facilities, but said at the time that they needed final approval from their superiors.

2007-10-03.title: Korean Leaders Meet in Pyongyang
2007-10-03.abstract: Leaders of North and South Korea sat down face-to-face today in the first summit meeting between the two Koreas in seven years.

2007-10-03.title: Colombian Leader Disputes Claim of Tie to Cocaine Kingpin
2007-10-03.abstract: President Álvaro Uribe of Colombia lashed out on Tuesday at claims in a new book that he had close ties to the late cocaine kingpin Pablo Escobar.

2007-10-03.title: As U.S. Food Dollars Buy Less, International Agencies Differ Over How to Use Aid
2007-10-03.abstract: Aid groups say that Congress should increase the food aid budget and use a larger share of it for long-term antipoverty programs.

2007-10-03.title: Maneuvering Before Vote in Pakistan
2007-10-03.abstract: On Tuesday, Gen. Pervez Musharraf named a close ally to succeed him as army chief and opposition politicians resigned to protest the coming elections.

2007-10-04.title: Monks Are Silenced, and for Now, Internet Is, Too
2007-10-04.abstract: After smuggled images jeopardized their crackdown on demonstrators, Myanmar’s generals cut off the Internet.

2007-10-04.title: Critic of Islam Confronts Dutch Over Guards
2007-10-04.abstract: Ayaan Hirsi Ali, an outspoken Dutch critic of Islam who moved to the United States to escape death threats in the Netherlands, has returned there to fight a Dutch government plan to cut off financing for her bodyguards.

2007-10-04.title: Pledging Peace, Koreans Agree on Economic Projects
2007-10-04.abstract: In a joint declaration today, South and North Korea said they would seek to put a formal end to the Korean War.

2007-10-04.title: U.S. ‘Not in Position’ to Attack, Iran Says
2007-10-04.abstract: Iran has concluded that the United States is overcommitted in Iraq and “not in a position to impose another war in the region against their taxpayers,” the Iranian foreign minister said.

2007-10-04.title: North Koreans Agree to Disable Nuclear Facilities
2007-10-04.abstract: The deal sets a timetable for North Korea to disable all of its nuclear facilities in return for economic aid.

2007-10-05.title: Human Terrain Teams
2007-10-05.abstract: An experimental Pentagon program pairs anthropologists and other social scientists with American combat units in Afghanistan and Iraq.

2007-10-05.title: Human Terrain
2007-10-05.abstract: In an experimental Pentagon program, a Human Terrain Team pairs anthropologists and other social scientists with American combat units in Afghanistan and Iraq.

2007-10-05.title: Gaza Strip: Hamas Accuses Fatah of Attack on Police
2007-10-05.abstract: A roadside bomb exploded near a car carrying a Hamas police patrol in Gaza, wounding at least three members of Hamas’s Executive Force. Hamas blamed Fatah for the attack, which came after Hamas took over the main Fatah office in Gaza City after the deaths of three Fatah gunmen when their car exploded late Tuesday. They were believed to be planning an attack on Hamas, which cited “security reasons” for seizing the Fatah building.

2007-10-05.title: Chilean Court Orders Arrests of Pinochet’s Kin and Close Allies
2007-10-05.abstract: A Chilean judge on Thursday ordered the arrests of the widow and five children of Gen. Augusto Pinochet and 17 of his closest military and civilian collaborators.

2007-10-05.title: South Africa Closes Mine That Trapped 3,200
2007-10-05.abstract: South African officials said that they had closed the huge Elandsrand mine for up to six weeks to determine the cause of the mishap.

2007-10-06.title: Christian Split in Lebanon Raises Specter of Civil War
2007-10-06.abstract: Many Lebanese worry that another civil war is imminent and some in its divided Christian community have begun re-establishing militias.

2007-10-06.title: Care of Dying Is Outlined by W.H.O.
2007-10-06.abstract: The document, aimed not at doctors, but at national health ministers, was the World Health Organization’s first guide to planning end-of-life care.

2007-10-06.title: Nepal Coalition’s Dispute With Former Rebels Stalls Elections
2007-10-06.abstract: The elections, which would have led to a new constitution, had been scheduled for Nov. 22. No new date has been set.

2007-10-06.title: Musharraf Wins Vote, but Court Will Have Final Say
2007-10-06.abstract: Gen. Pervez Musharraf easily won today’s presidential vote, but the election will be recognized only if the Supreme Court approves his eligibility.

2007-10-06.title: Joining Trend, Bulgaria Won’t Allow Prostitution
2007-10-06.abstract: The reversal comes as a growing number of European countries are imposing prostitution bans as a way to combat sexual trafficking.

2007-10-07.title: Russian Paper Publishing New Details of Journalist’s Killing
2007-10-07.abstract: Russian prosecutors know the identity of the man who killed a journalist in a contract-style killing, her newspaper is reporting.

2007-10-07.title: Okinawans Protest Japan’s Plan to Revise Bitter Chapter of World War II
2007-10-07.abstract: In new high school textbooks, the Japanese government is now denying that Okinawans had been coerced by troops into committing mass suicide during World War II.

2007-10-07.title: Syria Is Said to Be Strengthening Ties to Opponents of Iraq’s Government
2007-10-07.abstract: Syria is hoping to gain influence in Iraq before what it sees as the inevitable waning of the American presence there, diplomats and political analysts say.

2007-10-07.title: A Look at the Opposition in Myanmar
2007-10-07.abstract:
The generals’ main adversaries have been students,
civil servants, monks and political parties.

2007-10-07.title: For Balkan Shipping Agent, War Is Good for Business
2007-10-07.abstract: For most of his career, Tomislav Damnjanovic smuggled weapons to American opponents around the world, but since 2003 he’s played a crucial role in the U.S. wars in Iraq and Afghanistan.

2007-10-08.title: Blackwater Chief at Nexus of Military and Business
2007-10-08.abstract: Erik D. Prince, a former Navy Seal and scion of a wealthy family, has strong ties to the Republican Party.

2007-10-08.title: U.S. Calls Iranian Official Part of Elite Force
2007-10-08.abstract: The accusation that Iran’s ambassador to Iraq is an undisclosed member of Iran’s Revolutionary Guard came as the arrest of three suspected Iranian agents was announced.

2007-10-08.title: Gaza Rocket Is Said to Have Longer Range
2007-10-08.abstract: Palestinian militants fired a Katyusha rocket in a rare instance of the use of a more sophisticated missile than the usual crude, Gaza-made Qassams.

2007-10-08.title: Britain to Cut Its Force in Iraq by Half
2007-10-08.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown said that the number of British troops would fall to 2,500 from about 5,000 by next spring.

2007-10-08.title: Erik Hazelhoff Roelfzema, a ‘Soldier of Orange,’ Is Dead at 90
2007-10-08.abstract: Mr. Hazelhoff Roelfzema was acclaimed as one of the Netherlands’ greatest World War II heroes for leading missions against Nazi occupiers of his homeland.

2007-10-09.title:
Britain to Halve 
Its Force in Iraq
2007-10-09.abstract: The move suggests that the alliance on Iraq forged between former Prime Minister Tony Blair and Washington is unraveling.

2007-10-09.title: Costa Rica : Trade Pact Appears to Pass
2007-10-09.abstract: Voters in Costa Rica, Central America’s second biggest economy, backed a free trade agreement with the United States in a referendum Sunday. With 96.3 percent of the vote counted, 51.6 percent of voters backed the agreement, the independent Supreme Electoral Tribunal said on its Web site. President Óscar Arias, above, the winner of the 1987 Nobel Peace Prize, has staked much of his political agenda on passage of the agreement. A rejection would have been the first time a free trade deal with the United States was refused by any country. Before the voting began, the White House warned that the United States would not renegotiate the accord if it was voted down.

2007-10-09.title: Georgia: Ex-Minister in Televised About-Face
2007-10-09.abstract: Irakli Okruashvili, a prominent former government official, recanted on national television his claims that President Mikheil Saakashvili was corrupt and had committed grave crimes and cover-ups, including ordering the assassination of an Georgian oligarch. Mr. Okruashvili had held top posts in Mr. Saakashvili’s government, including defense minister, before declaring his opposition to the president. After making allegations against Mr. Saakashvili last month, Mr. Okruashvili was promptly arrested and charged with corruption. His turnabout on television was not immediately explained. His lawyer later said that she had not been allowed to attend the videotaped confession, raising questions about the confession’s credibility. Politicians loyal to Mr. Saakashvili suggested that Mr. Okruashvili had been plotting a coup, and that more arrests would be made.

2007-10-09.title: Students in Iran Protest President
2007-10-09.abstract: More than 100 pro-democracy students clashed with supporters of the president Monday at Tehran University.

2007-10-09.title: Mail Workers Resume Strike in Britain
2007-10-09.abstract: Seeking more pay and pension and job security, Royal Mail carriers and sorters began their second strike in less than a week on Monday.

2007-10-10.title: Faster Army Expansion Plan Approved
2007-10-10.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates has approved a plan to ease the strain of two wars on the military by increasing the size of the active-duty Army to 547,000 by 2010.

2007-10-10.title: India: In Search of Nigerian Crude
2007-10-10.abstract: Chasing China in resource-rich Africa, Prime Minister Manmohan Singh is to fly to Nigeria on Sunday in the first state visit there by an Indian prime minister in 45 years. Energy-starved India has been courting oil-rich African countries in recent years. Nigeria already provides for 11 percent of India’s crude oil supply, and Nalin Surie, a foreign ministry official, told reporters that India would seek deeper energy ties.

2007-10-10.title: Argentine Priest Receives Life Sentence in ‘Dirty War’ Killings
2007-10-10.abstract: An Argentine tribunal sentenced the Rev. Christian von Wernich for conspiring with the military in murders and kidnapping against leftist opponents.

2007-10-10.title: Political Paralysis Lets Pakistan Militants Thrive
2007-10-10.abstract: Three days of fierce fighting with pro-Taliban militants and foreign fighters have convulsed Pakistan’s tribal areas and left at least 45 Pakistani soldiers dead.

2007-10-10.title: German Wins Nobel in Chemistry
2007-10-10.abstract: Gerhard Ertl’s work in surface chemistry has applications across an array of fields, including the environment.

2007-10-11.title: Russia: Kremlin Feud Out in the Open
2007-10-11.abstract: A power struggle among the upper echelons of the Russian security services broke into the open this week when the head of the federal drug control service published an open letter in a prominent newspaper.

2007-10-11.title: Blackwater Case Highlights Legal Uncertainties
2007-10-11.abstract: While clear law exists for the military in war zones, a patchwork of untested laws and practical obstacles all but insulate contractors from accountability.

2007-10-11.title: West Bank: Israel Gives Residency Status to 3,500 Palestinians
2007-10-11.abstract: In a gesture to the Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas, Israel will grant residency permits to 3,500 Palestinians living illegally in the occupied West Bank but will not act on another 1,500 Palestinians living in Hamas-run Gaza, Israeli officials said.

2007-10-11.title: U.S. Military and Iraqis Say They Are Shut Out of Inquiry
2007-10-11.abstract: American military officials and Iraqi investigators say the F.B.I. and State Department are refusing to share information with them on their investigation into the Blackwater killings.

2007-10-11.title: 3,500 Palestinians in West Bank to Get Residency Permits
2007-10-11.abstract: Israel will grant residency permits to 3,500 Palestinians who have been living illegally in the occupied West Bank. Many are relatives of Palestinians who entered on travel visas.

2007-10-12.title: Nobel to Lessing, Incisive Voice of Women’s Fate
2007-10-12.abstract: Doris Lessing, the novelist whose deeply autobiographical writing has swept across continents and reflects her engagement with the social and political issues of her time, won the 2007 Nobel Prize in Literature.

2007-10-12.title: Party Power Struggle Enthralls South Africa
2007-10-12.abstract: Political and legal experts suspect that a brutal two-year battle for the power in the A.N.C. is spreading from within the party to the government itself.

2007-10-12.title: Suspect Denies Ties to German Bomb Plot
2007-10-12.abstract: Atilla Selek, a young German man with Turkish parents, stands at the center of the investigation in to a suspected terror plot uncovered last month, despite his denials.

2007-10-12.title: India: Bomb at Muslim Shrine Kills 2
2007-10-12.abstract: wo worshipers at one of India’s most revered Muslim shrines were killed when a bomb went off.

2007-10-12.title: In London, Gates Defends British Troop Cuts
2007-10-12.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said that the force reductions were being made with the full consent of the United States.

2007-10-13.title: New Evidence That Blackwater Guards Took No Fire
2007-10-13.abstract: Fresh accounts of a shooting in Baghdad last month cast further doubt on guards’ statements that they were responding to insurgents.

2007-10-13.title: Iranian Leader Decries Peace Session
2007-10-13.abstract: Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, called  for all Muslim countries to boycott an American-sponsored meeting for Middle East peace.

2007-10-13.title: Indian Prime Minister Avoiding Early Elections
2007-10-13.abstract: A nuclear agreement with the United States has stirred opposition from Communists in the Congress Party-led coalition government.

2007-10-13.title: Energy Crunch Threatens South American Nations
2007-10-13.abstract: An energy shortage in Brazil, Argentina and Chile is generating tensions between neighbors and emerging as one of the biggest brakes to growth and integration.

2007-10-13.title: U.S. Investigates Civilian Toll in Airstrike, but Holds Insurgents Responsible
2007-10-13.abstract: A strike on Thursday killed 15 civilians, one of the highest tolls from a single U.S. military action since the start of the Iraq war.

2007-10-14.title: Amid an Oil Boom, Poverty Persists
2007-10-14.abstract: After decades of civil war, Angola's economy is now exploding on the back of its oil and diamond wealth, but most Angolans haven't seen their living standards improve.

2007-10-14.title: After Rebuff, U.S. Visitors Reach Out to Russians
2007-10-14.abstract: The American secretaries of state and defense sought to show that the United States and Russia could find common ground, or at least outward civility.

2007-10-14.title: Iran’s Top Cleric Calls for Boycott of Peace Talks
2007-10-14.abstract: Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, called for all Muslim countries to boycott an American-sponsored meeting for Middle East peace.

2007-10-14.title: Rumblings in Spain Over Royal Family
2007-10-14.abstract: A recent series of small protests against King Juan Carlos of Spain have broken taboos and prompted debate about the role of the monarchy.

2007-10-14.title: Persecuted Sect in Iraq Avoids Its Shrine
2007-10-14.abstract: Nearly 15 percent of the 500,000-strong Yazidi sect in Iraq have fled the country as growing persecution has forced their religious observances underground.

2007-10-15.title: Forlorn Greens
2007-10-15.abstract: As Afghanistan struggles for a stable existence, so too does its only golf course.

2007-10-15.title: Israel Silent on Reports of Bombing Within Syria
2007-10-15.abstract: Neither American nor Israeli officials would confirm or deny a New York Times report that Israel had hit a Syrian nuclear site in a Sept. 6 airstrike.

2007-10-15.title: A Golf Course Where Water Is No Hazard
2007-10-15.abstract: Afghanistan’s only golf course is the result of one man’s dreamy vision nudged ever closer to a rugged reality.

2007-10-15.title: First Lady Raising Her Profile Without Changing Her Image
2007-10-15.abstract: Laura Bush, who once made her husband promise she would never have to give a speech, is stepping out in a new and unusually substantive way.

2007-10-15.title: Castro Speaks by Telephone With Chávez on TV
2007-10-15.abstract: The two leaders discussed plans to strengthen economic and political ties between Cuba and Venezuela.

2007-10-16.title: Crisis Looms in Eritrea
2007-10-16.abstract: Beneath the peace, harmony and South Beach style that once made Eritrea the little gem of Africa, cracks are beginning to show.

2007-10-16.title: Turkish Parliament to Vote on Iraq Action
2007-10-16.abstract: The Turkish government drafted a motion seeking parliamentary authority for the military to cross into Iraq to fight Kurdish separatist rebels.

2007-10-16.title: Resentment and Rations as Eritrea Nears a Crisis
2007-10-16.abstract: Facing rising prospects of war with Ethiopia and increasing tensions with the West, Eritrea has hit its most difficult point since winning its hard-fought independence 14 years ago.

2007-10-16.title: European Union Members Near Pact on Treaty
2007-10-16.abstract: A two-year political crisis in the European Union moved closer to resolution as differences narrowed over a treaty replacing the proposed constitution rejected by French and Dutch voters.

2007-10-16.title: Uzbekistan: Europeans Lift Some Sanctions
2007-10-16.abstract: European Union foreign ministers lifted a travel ban on eight top Uzbek officials while renewing an arms embargo.

2007-10-17.title: China Issues Warning on Dalai Lama Trip
2007-10-17.abstract: Chinese officials said a planned award ceremony in Washington for the Tibetan spiritual leader would have “an extremely serious impact” on relations.

2007-10-17.title: Olmert Defends Deal With Hezbollah
2007-10-17.abstract: Israel’s Prime Minister defended his decision to negotiate with Hezbollah as part of an effort to secure the return of two captured Israeli soldiers.

2007-10-17.title: Support Wanes in House for Genocide Vote
2007-10-17.abstract: The Turkish government has said House passage of the resolution would prompt it to reconsider its ties to the United States, including logistical support for the Iraq war.

2007-10-17.title: In Iran, Putin Warns Against Military Action
2007-10-17.abstract: At a meeting of Caspian Sea nations, President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia said that any use of force in the region was unacceptable.

2007-10-17.title: Pentagon Sees One Authority Over Contractors
2007-10-17.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates is pressing for the security firms in Iraq to fall under a single authority.

2007-10-18.title: A Language, Not Quite Spanish, With African Echoes
2007-10-18.abstract: Palenquero, still spoken in the jungle of northern Colombia, may be the last remnant of a Spanish-based lingua franca once used widely by slaves throughout Latin America.

2007-10-18.title: Iraqi Contracts With Iran and China Concern U.S.
2007-10-18.abstract: American officials fear that commercial investments in Iraqi power plants could mask military activities by Iran.

2007-10-18.title: Putin Is Said to Offer Idea on Standoff Over Iran
2007-10-18.abstract: Russia’s president, Vladimir V. Putin, proposed a new way to help resolve the standoff over Iran’s nuclear program during an extraordinary meeting with Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

2007-10-18.title: More Than 100 Dead in Pakistan Blasts
2007-10-18.abstract: Two explosions went off near a truck carrying Benazir Bhutto, Pakistan’s former prime minister, who returned from exile to her home city today. Ms. Bhutto was not injured in the blasts.

2007-10-18.title: Key Human Traits Tied to Shellfish Remains
2007-10-18.abstract: An international team of scientists have found evidence that suggests that Homo sapiens developed a taste for shellfish much earlier than previously thought.

2007-10-19.title: Turkish Bid to Pursue Kurds Poses Quandary for Iraq
2007-10-19.abstract: Turkey’s decision to allow the dispatch of troops over Iraq’s border in pursuit of Kurdish guerrillas throws into relief a troubling quandary for Iraq’s leaders.

2007-10-19.title: Shiite Refugees Feel Forsaken in Their Holy City
2007-10-19.abstract: A visit to Najaf lays bare the needs of displaced Iraqis and the rough road ahead for national reconciliation.

2007-10-19.title: Leaders of Venezuela and Colombia, Ideological Opposites, Are Tightening Ties
2007-10-19.abstract: Colombia and Venezuela are rapidly moving to strike energy deals, resolve a boundary dispute and increase trade.

2007-10-19.title: Neanderthals Had Important Speech Gene, DNA Evidence Shows
2007-10-19.abstract: New evidence that Neanderthals possessed a critical gene known to underlie speech, stems from analysis of a gene called FOXP2, which is associated with language.

2007-10-19.title: Bolivia: Soldiers Take Over Main Airport
2007-10-19.abstract: Air Force troops seized control of the nation’s busiest international airport, in the eastern city of Santa Cruz, after the airport’s managers detained several planes on the runway.

2007-10-20.title: Rocking the Vote
2007-10-20.abstract: Polish youth are using video to help the nation's low voter turnout.

2007-10-20.title: Backstage, U.S. Nurtured Pakistan Rivals’ Deal
2007-10-20.abstract: The violence upon Benazir Bhutto’s return has raised questions about whether a tenuous power-sharing deal with President Pervez Musharraf can survive.

2007-10-20.title: Blast at Mall Kills 8 in Philippines
2007-10-20.abstract: Eight people were killed and as many as 130 others wounded Friday when a powerful explosion ripped through a shopping mall in Manila.

2007-10-20.title: Iran’s Nuclear Envoy Resigns Ahead of Talks
2007-10-20.abstract: The resignation of the negotiator, seen as a moderating influence in Tehran, dimmed hopes for this week’s talks.

2007-10-20.title: After Bombing, Bhutto Assails Officials’ Ties
2007-10-20.abstract: The opposition leader Benazir Bhutto said she had warned the Pakistani government that suicide bomb squads were going to go after her on her return to the country.

2007-10-21.title: Iraq President Assails Syria’s Support for Turkish Cross-Border Threat
2007-10-21.abstract: President Jalal Talabani of Iraq said that Syria’s president had crossed a “red line” by speaking approvingly of Turkey’s threat of a cross-border offensive.

2007-10-21.title: Two Killed in Gaza Fight Between Clan and Hamas
2007-10-21.abstract: A 13-year-old boy was one of two people killed in fighting between the Hamas police and a Gaza clan aligned with the Fatah movement. In addition, 20 were wounded.

2007-10-21.title: Wired Youth Aim to Sway Poland’s Vote
2007-10-21.abstract: A text message spread between cellphones imploring Polish youths to steal their grandmothers’ IDs highlights  the power conservative older women have at the polls.

2007-10-21.title: Kisho Kurokawa, Japanese Architect Who Pioneered Organic Structures, Dies at 73
2007-10-21.abstract: Mr. Kurokawa was one of the youngest founding members of Japan’s Metabolist movement.

2007-10-21.title: Marine Inquiry Into Afghan Killings to Look at 2 Officers, Lawyer Says
2007-10-21.abstract: A Marine Corps inquiry investigating the killings of at least a dozen Afghan civilians last March will only focus on the actions of two officers in charge.

2007-10-22.title: Chávez’s Plan for Development Bank Moves Ahead
2007-10-22.abstract: Doubts persist about the need for a bank to finance regional development projects, a plan advanced by Hugo Chávez, Venezuela’s president.

2007-10-22.title: 5 Arrested in Killing of Reggae Star
2007-10-22.abstract: The arrests come three days after the reggae star, Lucky Dube, was shot in a botched attempt to steal his late-model Chrysler.

2007-10-22.title: Cheney, Like President, Has a Warning for Iran
2007-10-22.abstract: Vice President Dick Cheney on Sunday said Iran would face “serious consequences” if the government there does not abandon its nuclear program.

2007-10-22.title: Far-Right Party Posts Gains in Voting for Swiss Parliament
2007-10-22.abstract: After a campaign widely criticized as racist, Switzerland’s far-right strengthened its position as the biggest single party in Parliament in a general election on Sunday.

2007-10-22.title: Politburo in China Gets Four New Members
2007-10-22.abstract: The changes in the Communist Party’s leadership lineup modestly enhance the authority President Hu Jintao.

2007-10-23.title: Kurdish Guerrillas at War With Iran
2007-10-23.abstract: Out of the public eye, Kurdish rebels have been waging a deadly insurgency in Iran.

2007-10-23.title: Lebanon: Presidential Election Postponed
2007-10-23.abstract: Lebanon delayed the election of a successor to the pro-Syrian president Émile Lahoud by members of Parliament by almost three weeks.

2007-10-23.title: Bush Asks Congress for $1.4 Billion to Fight Drugs in Mexico
2007-10-23.abstract: President Bush asked Congress on Monday to approve a $1.4 billion aid package over the next two years to help the Mexican government fight narcotics traffickers.

2007-10-23.title: Brazil: Retrial in U.S. Nun’s Killing
2007-10-23.abstract: The retrial of Rayfran das Neves Sales, sentenced to 27 years in prison in 2005 for the shooting death earlier that year of Dorothy Stang.

2007-10-23.title: Students in Iran Protest Sentences for 3 Activists
2007-10-23.abstract: Students from different universities gathered amid high security to protest jail terms for three student activists, accused of publishing articles insulting to Islam.

2007-10-24.title: 4 Palestinian Militants Die in Raids and Jail Riot
2007-10-24.abstract: Three Palestinian militants were killed by Israeli Army fire on Tuesday and a Palestinian prisoner died of wounds sustained Monday during the quelling of a riot in an Israeli prison.

2007-10-24.title: Bhutto Receives New Death Threat, Aide Says
2007-10-24.abstract: Adding to concerns about the security of the opposition leader Benazir Bhutto, a close aide said Tuesday that she had received a new death threat, in the form of a letter.

2007-10-24.title: Bush Stands by Plan for Missile Defenses
2007-10-24.abstract: President Bush defended his plans for missile defenses, arguing that Iran posed a threat to some NATO allies.

2007-10-24.title: State Department Use of Contractors Leaps in 4 Years
2007-10-24.abstract: The amount of money the department pays to private contractors has soared, but it has added few officials to oversee the contracts.

2007-10-24.title: Iran Has New Nuclear Negotiator, but Similar Stance
2007-10-24.abstract: Saeed Jalili, Iran’s new chief nuclear negotiator, made his international debut in Rome on Tuesday, meeting with the European Union foreign policy chief, Javier Solana.

2007-10-25.title: China Says Economy Grew at 11.5% Pace
2007-10-25.abstract: The pace of China’s economic development is still strong enough to keep pushing up prices around the world.

2007-10-25.title: Russia Working to Limit Election Observers
2007-10-25.abstract: Russia has begun an effort to curtail the activities of election observers in the states of the former Soviet Union.

2007-10-25.title: Darfur Talks in Danger of Lacking Attendance
2007-10-25.abstract: The U.N. envoy for Darfur on Wednesday urged fractious rebel groups and the Sudanese government to overcome their internal differences and attend a peace conference scheduled to begin in three days.

2007-10-25.title: Kyrgyzstan: Journalist Shot Dead
2007-10-25.abstract: A journalist who reported extensively for the Voice of America was shot to death outside his office in Osh, near the border with Uzbekistan.

2007-10-25.title: Congo’s Army Clashing With Militias
2007-10-25.abstract: Battles between rival militias are driving thousands of beleaguered villagers from their homes and complicating the government’s efforts to strike a truce.

2007-10-26.title: The Blackwater Shooting
2007-10-26.abstract: Witnesses help reconstruct the killing of 17 Iraqis by American contractors in Baghdad.

2007-10-26.title: Italy: Beleaguered Premier Demands a Clear Show of Unity
2007-10-26.abstract: With internal cracks and dissension widening, Prime Minister Romano Prodi called for a declaration of support among the diverse partners in his fragile center-left government. “It’s time for the political forces in the ruling majority to say clearly whether they intend to continue supporting the government or if they intend instead to put their own interests above the country’s,” Mr. Prodi said on national television. He spoke amid rising concern that the year-old coalition, ranging from communists to centrist Catholics, might soon crumble over disagreement on his proposed budget and fighting among ministers. His predecessor, Silvio Berlusconi, now the opposition leader, is calling for new elections.

2007-10-26.title: Photos Show Cleansing of Suspect Syrian Site
2007-10-26.abstract: A Syrian site that Israel bombed last month no longer bears any obvious traces of what analysts said appeared to have been a partly built nuclear reactor.

2007-10-26.title: U.S. Plays Its ‘Unilateral’ Card on Iran Sanctions
2007-10-26.abstract: After 18 months in which the Bush administration has touted the virtues of collective action against Iran, the new sanctions mark a major turn toward unilateralism.

2007-10-26.title: A Monk’s Tale of Protest and Escape From Myanmar
2007-10-26.abstract: Ashin Kovida is 24-year-old Buddhist monk who says he was one of the leaders of the recent protests in Myanmar and escaped last week to Thailand.

2007-10-27.title: Kazakh Web Sites Blocked
2007-10-27.abstract: Site were temporarily shut down this week, organizers said, for publishing documents relating to the battle between the country’s leader and his estranged former son-in-law.

2007-10-27.title: Empty Seats Add to Worry on Eve of Darfur Talks
2007-10-27.abstract: United Nations and African Union officials on Friday expressed disappointment that several rebel leaders had not showed up, but they said talks had to begin anyway.

2007-10-27.title: Yet Another Photo of Site in Syria, Yet More Questions
2007-10-27.abstract: The mystery surrounding the construction of what might have been a nuclear reactor in Syria deepened Friday, when a company released a satellite photo from September 2003.

2007-10-27.title: The Defender of a Lesser-Known Guarantee in Russia
2007-10-27.abstract: Ivan Y. Pavlov leads a quiet but dangerous campaign in Russia: legal battles for what he calls, simply, “the right to know.”

2007-10-27.title: Iraq Plan to Add U.S. Troops at Kurdish Border Is Rejected by Turkey
2007-10-27.abstract: Turkey’s prime minister on Friday rejected an Iraqi proposal that included a military role for the U.S. in resolving a standoff over raids by Kurdish guerrillas.

2007-10-28.title: Greenland Thaws
2007-10-28.abstract: A warmer climate means that Greenland's vast white landscape could someday be full of vegetable farms and viable forests.

2007-10-28.title: Clans Complicate Philippine Conflict
2007-10-28.abstract: Clan violence has aggravated the conflict between government forces and Islamic separatists in the southern Philippines, making the decade-long search for peace there even harder, a new study says.

2007-10-28.title: Warming Revives Flora and Fauna in Greenland
2007-10-28.abstract: As the climate warms, Greenlandic farmers are experimenting with vegetables that have previously never been grown in the country.

2007-10-28.title: Kazakh Web Sites Blocked in Leader’s Family Feud
2007-10-28.abstract: Opposition Web sites in Kazakhstan were temporarily shut down, organizers said, for publishing documents relating to the public battle between the country’s authoritarian leader and his estranged former son-in-law.

2007-10-28.title: Sudan Declares Cease-Fire at Darfur Peace Talks
2007-10-28.abstract: The Sudanese government declared a unilateral cease-fire at peace talks on Darfur, but because rebels boycotted the talks, it was not clear if this was a breakthrough moment.

2007-10-29.title: Rebels Split at Talks on Darfur
2007-10-29.abstract: At peace talks in Libya, some Darfur rebels stressed unity with the rest of Sudan while others threatened to split off and create their own country.

2007-10-29.title: Israel Reduces Fuel to Gaza, Closes Crossing
2007-10-29.abstract: The action is in line with a recent government decision to respond with sanctions to continued rocket fire from the Hamas-run territory.

2007-10-29.title: Petraeus Says U.S. Seeking Calm in North
2007-10-29.abstract: The American military’s commander in Iraq indicated that behind-the-scenes efforts were under way to calm the situation on the border between Iraq and Turkey.

2007-10-29.title: Turkey Attacks Kurdish Rebel Positions
2007-10-29.abstract: Special forces troops, supported by helicopter gunships, assaulted the rebel positions in the southeastern province of Tunceli.

2007-10-29.title: India’s Peasants Protest for Land Rights
2007-10-29.abstract: Thousands of peasants marched more than 200 miles to New Delhi demanding land reform.

2007-10-30.title: Immunity Deals Offered to Blackwater Guards
2007-10-30.abstract: Officials said that State Department investigators lacked the authority to offer the deals.

2007-10-30.title: Foreign Fighters of Harsher Bent Bolster Taliban
2007-10-30.abstract: The growing numbers of foreign fighters in Afghanistan are more violent and extreme than their local allies.

2007-10-30.title: Bhutto’s Return Brings Pakistani Politics to a Boil
2007-10-30.abstract: Home for just over a week, Benazir Bhutto is rallying supporters and courting the news media,  remaining a polarizing figure.

2007-10-30.title: Egypt: Mubarak Revives Nuclear Program
2007-10-30.abstract: President Hosni Mubarak announced plans to build several nuclear reactor, following Iran’s lead.

2007-10-30.title: Argentina’s First Lady Elected President
2007-10-30.abstract: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner has become the first woman to be elected president in Argentina’s history.

2007-10-31.title: Darfur Peace Conference
2007-10-31.abstract: Unity is scarce as rebel leaders from Darfur convene in Libya.

2007-10-31.title: Report Says Army Buys Boy Soldiers in Myanmar
2007-10-31.abstract: The military in Myanmar, struggling to meet recruiting quotas, is buying, kidnapping and terrorizing boys as young as 10 to fill its ranks.

2007-10-31.title: Putin Mourns Victims at Stalin-Era Killing Field
2007-10-31.abstract: The Russian president visited a memorial at a site where thousands were executed, as Russia marked the 70th anniversary of Joseph Stalin’s Great Terror.

2007-10-31.title: Iraqi Cabinet Votes to End Security Firms’ Immunity
2007-10-31.abstract: Draft legislation that lifts immunity for foreign private security companies gained the consent of the Iraqi cabinet on Tuesday.

2007-10-31.title: Indonesia Upholds Death in Drug Cases
2007-10-31.abstract: The Constitutional Court of Indonesia upheld the death penalty for serious drug offenses on Tuesday, dimming hopes of a reprieve for three Australians facing execution.
